name: Brandon Quester
subject_line: Data: Arizona's pedestrian fatality rate is 3.6 per 100
personalization_paragraph: Maricopa pedestrian deaths cluster on a handful of named arterials, but the citywide headline doesn't capture the intersection pattern. The data breaks out left-turn fatalities, wide-arterial clusters, and the corridors where ADOT redesigns haven't moved the curve.
asset_stat: Arizona's pedestrian fatality rate is 3.6 per 100,000 residents, nearly twice the national average; 76 percent of pedestrian deaths happen in darkness on wide arterials designed for vehicle speed, not pedestrian safety
